author: TheFermiSea
text: Deployed architecture swarm on ai-proxy from /home/brian/code/beefcake-arch-swarm-20260312-232012 at commit 58c0eaa. Imported local .beads/issues.jsonl into the deploy checkout because ai-proxy local beads/BeadHub feed could not see beefcake-f6ur*. Direct bd lookups in the deploy checkout resolve beefcake-f6ur through beefcake-f6ur.3 correctly. Autonomous supervisor is running as PID 777453 via /home/brian/arch-swarm-supervisor.sh with 10-minute polling. Current phase: beefcake-f6ur.1 in_progress under worker-first mode. Supervisor log: latest /home/brian/arch-swarm-supervisor-*.log. Run log: latest /home/brian/code/beefcake-arch-swarm-20260312-232012/logs/dogfood/arch-beefcake-f6ur.1-*.log. Runtime note: using direct bd rather than bdh for issue selection/tracking on ai-proxy because bdh ready there returns unrelated BeadHub task feed instead of the imported architecture beads.

author: TheFermiSea
text: Validated that .beads/metadata.json is in legacy SQLite-shaped form (database=beads.db, missing backend=dolt), which explains why bd doctor still probes stale paths while normal bd calls succeed. Current tracked .beads/backup/* reflects the live beefcake Dolt dataset; untracked .beads/issues.jsonl is the wrong exported dataset and should not be preserved.

author: TheFermiSea
text: Residual after beefcake-n59r: local bd doctor is healthy overall, but the local Dolt data dir still contains the legacy beads_beefcake-swarm database and bd vc/auto-push still report non-fast-forward against origin/main. This did not keep the git worktree dirty anymore, but it still needs Beads/Dolt hygiene work.

author: TheFermiSea
text: Delegating to ai-proxy swarm for an autonomous worker-first test run. Target is bounded to crates/swarm-agents/src/tools/fs_tools.rs and is intended to exercise real read/edit/tool coordination while improving swarm freshness guards.

author: TheFermiSea
text: Launching autonomous swarm test from clean local checkout in direct-beads, worker-first mode. Goal: validate a bounded real task that exercises read/edit/verifier flow without ai-proxy tracker contamination.

author: TheFermiSea
text: Investigated ai-proxy tracker failure. Root cause: server-mode Beads/Dolt contamination on ai-proxy. /home/brian/code/beefcake-swarm/.beads/dolt hosts a long-lived shared dolt sql-server on port 3307, and other checkouts/deploys attach to it instead of their local tracker state. The live DB currently serves unrelated bd-* issues while .beads/issues.jsonl in beefcake checkouts is correct. This blocks trustworthy server-side swarm issue resolution until the shared beads database is rebuilt or isolated.

author: TheFermiSea
text: ai-proxy cannot currently resolve beefcake issues through bd because its shared server-mode Dolt database is contaminated. Launching the actual swarm work there via --issue-file while preserving canonical issue state here locally as in_progress and monitoring the run manually.

author: TheFermiSea
text: Active autonomous swarm run launched on ai-proxy via --issue-file bridge. PID 1071736, log /home/brian/z7vk-ai-proxy-20260313-130750.log, worktree /tmp/beefcake-wt/beefcake-z7vk. Using worker-first local-model mode because this laptop cannot reach vasp endpoints directly.
